description: A pair of bandits blocks a narrow stone bridge, demanding 10 gold to pass. A frightened traveller stands at your side.
objective: Get past the bandits and cross the bridge
customIntroduction: You and a frightened traveller stand at the near end of a narrow stone bridge spanning a dark gorge. Two cloaked figures block the far end — armed, still, and watching. One steps forward and raises a hand. "Ten gold," he growls, "or you don't cross." The traveller edges close beside you and whispers: "I'm with you — whatever you decide."
customContext: Encounter: Bridge Toll Standoff. Two bandits are blocking a narrow stone bridge, demanding 10 gold to pass. A frightened traveller stands alongside the player as an ally and will fight with them if combat breaks out. MECHANICS — Before combat: if the player refuses to pay, call set_disposition on both bandit npcs (to "enemy"), then trigger_combat. If the player agrees to pay 10 gold, call award_gold with amount -10, call set_world_flag with name "bridge_toll_paid" and value true (this disarms the cross-the-bridge ambush trigger), keep bandits neutral, and narrate them stepping aside — no combat. During combat: if the player surrenders and offers 20 gold, call award_gold with amount -20, call set_world_flag with name "bridge_toll_paid" and value true, call set_disposition on both bandits to "neutral", then call end_combat — the bandits take the money and let everyone pass. AMBUSH TRIGGER: an encounter trigger (`bridge_crossed_without_paying`) auto-fires if the player walks onto the far half of the bridge while `bridge_toll_paid` is unset — the bandits become hostile and combat starts. Roleplay the bandits as gruff, opportunistic brigands — firm but not fanatical. They want money, not a fight. CHAPTER COMPLETION — When the standoff is resolved (the player has paid, the bandits have been defeated in combat, or the situation has otherwise concluded with the player past the bridge), call set_world_flag with name "bridge_resolved" and value true. This marks the chapter complete in adventure mode. Call it once, at the moment the resolution is unambiguous in the fiction; combat victory will also auto-complete the chapter on its own, so only set the flag for non-combat resolutions or when narrating the post-combat aftermath.

then.message: The player tried to walk past the bandits without paying. Both bandits draw weapons and combat begins.
